As a workaround that has helped many users, please go to device Settings, Apps, our app, tap Force Stop and Clear Cache, then uninstall, reboot the device, and reinstall the latest version from the store. If the crash continues, please email our support team with your device model, Android version, and app version, ideally with a screenshot of the hardware acceleration message, so our engineers can pinpoint the rendering path causing the issue. We really appreciate the detail you provided, it helps us track this down.

review_text: always wrong direction . assign very far driver . much delay before start the trip . i always need to call driver to give him direction because of wrong map . the service could be great if there be not such fatal mistake .
A_response: Hi, thank you for the detailed feedback, and we're truly sorry about the wrong directions, far driver assignments, and the delays you've experienced. Please note that trip requests are first sent to the nearest available driver partner, and if not accepted they get forwarded to the next nearest one, which can sometimes mean a slightly longer wait, though your fare is not affected. The map and routing issues you describe definitely shouldn't be happening, so we'd like to investigate this for you. Could you open a ticket at <url>/contact with your trip details (date, time, pickup location) so we can review the specific cases and follow up with you? We appreciate your patience and want to make this right.
B_response: We're sorry your trips have been disrupted by wrong directions and far-away driver assignments; you should not have to call the driver to guide them to you. The behavior you're describing points to GPS lock loss and routing inaccuracies in our map and driver-matching service, and the trip request gets forwarded to the next available driver if the closest one does not accept, which can sometimes increase the distance. To help us investigate, please open a ticket at <url>contact with the trip ID, your pickup location, and a screenshot of the map at booking time. We will follow up from there and use the data to improve matching accuracy.

B_response: We're sorry App Lock isn't working properly on your Galaxy S4 and we'd love to get it sorted so you can give that 5-star rating. To help us troubleshoot, could you let us know whether App Lock fails for every app or just some, and check a couple of settings: open App Lock options and confirm the re-lock mode (after screen off or after quitting the app), and make sure "Usage Access" is enabled for the app in your phone's settings. If it's still not working after that, please email us at our support address with screenshots and your app version so we can investigate further.

review_text: the <app> seem okay at first but then i find several drawback for it . <digit> . the <app> setting do not work properly . <digit> . the <app> drawer be a mess with no way to arrange the apps . <digit> . any newly instal <app> would create a new home screen to store the <app> shortcut . there be no way to disable this . totally the <app> management part be a mess with this launcher . the only good thing be it add a few of the clean master functionality into the launcher . but this be redundant if you already have <app> instal .
A_response: Thank you for the detailed feedback, this is exactly the kind of report that helps us improve the home-screen and app-drawer management. For the new home screen being created on every install, please open Launcher Settings > Desktop Settings and toggle off 'Add icon to home screen' for newly installed apps, which should stop the auto-page creation. For the drawer, long-press an icon and choose 'Sort' to switch to a manual ordering mode where you can drag icons freely. We are noting your point about settings not persisting, and if you can email us your device model and launcher version, we will follow up so the management features feel less cluttered for you.
B_response: Thank you for the detailed feedback, this is exactly the kind of input that helps us improve. On the app drawer, you can long-press any icon and drag it to rearrange or drop it onto another icon to create a folder, which should help organize things. For the new home screen being created automatically with each install, this can usually be controlled in the launcher's Advanced Settings, please make sure you're on the latest version, as we've been refining these options. If the launcher settings still aren't behaving correctly for you, please email our support team with your device model and app version so we can investigate. We really appreciate you taking the time to list each issue.

review_text: not get small window mode feature update to latest version . of uc browser .
A_response: Thank you for sticking with UC Browser and for raising this. We hear you on wanting a cleaner browsing experience and the small window / read mode you remember from earlier versions, especially across the homepage and UC News integration. As a workaround, you can reduce news cards by long-pressing them on the homepage to hide them, and you can mute UC News push notifications via Menu > Settings > Notifications. We are tracking the request for a single master toggle to disable UC News content and an optional small-window mode, and we appreciate you helping us prioritize it.
B_response: Hi there, we're sorry for the trouble with the small window mode in the latest version. This feature is currently in a limited test rollout, so it has only been released to a subset of users for now, but we plan to roll it out more widely as soon as testing is complete. We appreciate your patience while we finish that work. If you run into any other issues in the meantime, please reach out via the Menu > Feedback option inside UC Browser and we'll be glad to help.

review_text: add fast forward and fast backward button in video player also include option for add subtitle file to video .
A_response: Thanks for the thoughtful suggestions on the video player, specifically fast-forward/rewind controls and subtitle-file import. Both are great additions to the playback and editor toolset, and we've logged them along with related asks like next/previous track buttons and original-audio removal. In the meantime, you can already load .srt subtitle files in many of our player views via the subtitle search dialog, and we're happy to walk you through it if you share your device model. Please send any further detail to our support email so we can pass it directly to the development team.
B_response: Hi, thanks so much for the thoughtful suggestions on fast-forward/rewind buttons and adding subtitle files. For loading subtitles today, you can open a video and use the subtitle search dialog, or place the subtitle file in the same folder as your video so the player picks it up automatically. We've passed your request for dedicated FF/RW controls along for consideration in a future update. If you run into any specific issues with subtitle loading, please share details on our forum so the team can take a closer look.
